description: classifyCurrentSession silently returns { kind: 'no-current' } when EditingState or SaveFailedState is provided with currentNote===null (lines 35-37 and 50-52). The spec classification table in REQ-EPNS-007 defines EditingState with Note (non-null) and SaveFailedState with Note (non-null) as the only valid input combinations. currentNote===null is only valid for IdleState. Returning 'no-current' for an EditingState with null note is dangerous: it causes the pipeline to skip the flush step entirely, potentially losing unsaved content. This should throw an error (consistent with the saving/switching guard at lines 60-66) rather than silently misclassifying.
specReference: REQ-EPNS-007 classification table: EditingState requires Note (non-null), SaveFailedState requires Note (non-null)
